ops.text: The capt-hook Workflow-routing guard re-flags the sol-first-with-fable-fallback review pattern on every dispatch (5+ times this session): workflow scripts route finder/refuter stages to agentType 'codex:codex-wrapper' as primary and only fall back to an unpinned (fable-inheriting) agent() inside a try/catch when the codex agent type is unregistered (plugin disconnected) or returns empty — which is CLAUDE.md's own 'escalation means fable' rung. The guard pattern-matches any unpinned agent() near review-shaped labels and cannot see the conditional routing, so each dispatch costs a hook round-trip and a justification paragraph. Guard fix: when a script contains both agentType:'codex:codex-wrapper' and an unpinned agent() for the same stage key (fallback shape), treat the stage as sol-routed; or downgrade the flag to a nudge when the codex plugin is currently disconnected.
